response:
Bishop Godfrey Mary Paul Okoye is remembered as the first Catholic Bishop of Enugu and as one of the key builders of the Church’s institutional foundation in what became the Enugu Diocese. His name is carried by Godfrey Okoye University because his life and ministry strongly emphasized education, formation of conscience, discipline, and service to society.

Early life and priestly formation are usually described in Church records in terms of his vocation, seminary training, and early pastoral assignments. As a priest, he belonged to the generation that helped consolidate Catholic structures in Eastern Nigeria through parish work, catechesis, and the training of lay leaders. Those who worked with him often highlight his seriousness about order, standards, and the long-term development of people rather than short-term popularity.

Episcopal leadership and the Enugu Diocese are central to his biography. As the first bishop of the diocese, he had the foundational responsibility of shaping its identity and systems: organizing parishes and pastoral zones, strengthening clergy formation and discipline, promoting Catholic education, and building administrative structures that could outlive his tenure. “First bishop” in this context is significant because it means many of the policies, institutions, and expectations that later bishops inherited were initially set under his leadership.

A major thread in accounts of his legacy is education. He is frequently associated with the conviction that the Church’s mission includes forming the mind and the character: schools that combine academic excellence with moral formation, respect for human dignity, and responsibility to the community. This educational vision is the reason the Diocese of Enugu later chose to name a university after him, presenting the institution as a continuation of the values he stood for.

His pastoral emphasis is typically described as faith formation and service. That includes strengthening sacramental life, encouraging catechetical instruction, supporting religious communities, and promoting practical charity. In many dioceses of that era, bishops also played a visible role in mediating social tensions and encouraging peace and civic responsibility; he is commonly remembered within that tradition of episcopal public witness.

In remembrance, Bishop Okoye is spoken of as a leader who valued discipline, clarity of purpose, and building durable institutions. For Godfrey Okoye University, this translates into an identity that stresses academic seriousness, ethical standards, and service-oriented leadership.
